reviews.title: Great Little Hydration Pack
reviews.text: I bought this for short (~3 hr) summer hikes in the mid-Atlantic region, where I need a lot of water.  The 2L hydration pack was perfect for that.  The bladder works well, the connector to the hose is solid.  The only "trick" was that I have to thread the hose through all of the holders on the strap and into the bag itself before connecting it to the bladder, since the mouthpiece is too large to thread though.  The bladder sits in an insulated section of the pack, so it holds the cool temperature pretty well, even better if you dump some ice in before you leave home.  What I really like is how much other stuff fits in the pack:  tissues, hand sanitizer, headband, my huge wallet, and a hat (and I don't even stuff it full).  The laces on the outside of the pack would even accommodate a light jacket.  There are two hip pockets, neither of which is large enough for my iPhone 8 plus without having to really work it in there, but it great for chapstick and my car keys (there is no clip inside for keys, as I have in other packets, but one of the hip band pockets works just fine for this)!  This is an ideal alternative to my large Osprey pack when I need to take a lot of water with me on a hike and I don't want to wear a large pack.  The pack also comes with a little flexible cleaner for the hose and with an extra gasket for the hose-bladder connection.  Great buy!

reviews.title: Small, lightweight, foldable day pack
reviews.text:
My husband was looking for a day pack. I bought him 6 backpacks to compare. The [[ASIN:B010LAU5CG Hiking Cycling Backpack, Sunhiker Sports Outdoor Backpack Bag Running Camping Backpack Water Resistant Lightweight SMALL Daypack 25L M441 (Black)]], the [[ASIN:B00DNTR5CQ Osprey Packs Momentum 22 Daypack (Spring 2016 Model), Carbide Grey]], the [[ASIN:B014EMLHDI Outlander Packable Handy Lightweight Travel Hiking Backpack Daypack, Grey]], the [[ASIN:B00FUWSTI8 Lightweight Rucksack 25L for men women kids - Foldable into Inner pocket (BLACK)]], the [[ASIN:B013K7CJD0 Cycling Hiking Backpack Sunhiker Water Resistant Travel Backpack Lightweight SMALL Daypack M0714 (Dark Green)]], and the [[ASIN:B01EH0YGSU Free Knight 25L Packable Handy Lightweight Travel Backpack Daypack-Lifetime Warranty (New Grey)]]. There were pros and cons for each pack. He decided to keep the [[ASIN:B010LAU5CG Hiking Cycling Backpack, Sunhiker Sports Outdoor Backpack Bag Running Camping Backpack Water Resistant Lightweight SMALL Daypack 25L M441 (Black)]]because it met his needs the best. We ordered all packs in gray, green, or black. We returned the other 5 packs. I liked 2 of the lightweight foldable packs - the [[ASIN:B014EMLHDI Outlander Packable Handy Lightweight Travel Hiking Backpack Daypack, Grey]]and the [[ASIN:B01EH0YGSU Free Knight 25L Packable Handy Lightweight Travel Backpack Daypack-Lifetime Warranty (New Grey)]]. I decided to order those for myself in Pink and Purple.
All of the packs had a mesh pocket on each side of the pack for water bottles. We tested them all with a Nalgene bottle and a bottle of water. All of the packs could easily hold the bottle of water. Some were a very tight fit for the Nalgene bottles. All packs had a main compartment with one or two pockets in the front of the pack. The main compartments varied from 561 cubic inches to 1386 cubic inches. 3 of the packs were lightweight, foldable, and storable in their own pocket.
Here are my summaries of this pack:
825 cubic inches
16 1/2” x 10” x 5”
6” mesh pockets – smaller pockets
Covers about 4 ¾” of bottles
Straps 2 ¼” wide
This pack has 3 main compartments. In the back is the largest zippered compartment. In the middle is a middle sized zippered compartment. In the front is a small zippered pocket.
Pros:
- Small zippered pocket inside the main compartment. This is where the pack is stored when folded.
- Very easy to adjust shoulder straps
- Reinforced mesh straps feel soft and pliable
- Pockets fit a Nalgene bottle
- Handle at top of pack for an additional way of carrying pack
Cons:
- Thinner material than a standard backpack.
- No back padding and did not keep it’s shape. Typical for a foldable pack.

I ordered one of these for myself in a different color.

All around, a great backpack that I absolutely recommend!

reviews.title: No structure but pretty
reviews.text:
I really wanted to love this backpack.
I don't even like it let alone love it. A coworker has it and loves it. I'm not a backpacker but even if I were I'm not sure I'd like this style. I think it would be too tall and without structure I'd spend half my hike digging things out. I wanted something pretty to carry to work after my workhorse gave up so in I went. This pack has a pear shape and is tall. Without structure I found it difficult to put things in any one of the many pockets even with my lunch bag as an anchor. The bag just flops over. I gave up and grabbed a reusable grocery bag to carry things today. The slick material slipped right off my shoulder when I trialed that. I get it because it's waterproof. I don't carry with one shoulder but in case you do this doesn't work well that way. If you like non structured bags this would be a good deal. It just doesn't work for me. 3 stars because even though it terrible for me it seems to be a decent bag. And pretty.

Likes:
Very pretty
Bag well made, the mesh pockets IMO maybe not so much
Waterproof-Seattle after all ☔
Good number of pockets

Dislikes:
Zero structure
Very tall
A-line
Slick material not conducive to one shoulder carry

reviews.title: Half price, half excellence?
reviews.text:
I took my SHARKMOUTH Tactical MOLLE Hydration Pack for a 4 mile, first outing today &...it doesn't suck. The dregs from 2 liters of iced water still held a few cubes after 1-1/2 hours in sunny, mid 80s weather. Bladder was free of leaks, bad odor or bad flavor. The first flaw was noted while loading the filled bladder in the insulated pocket. The velcro tabs to hold up the bladder are haphazardly sewn in rather than aligned with the slots on the bladder. They still manage to prevent "slouch in the pouch." The bite valve dust cover came off repeatedly. The bite valve leaks enough that I had to suck air from the drinking tube every time. Aside from the velcro misplacement the stitching & materials look good. Compared to the starting prices of C@melB@k gear this isn't a bad deal. Is it?
To provide perspective for the attached photos I'm a leggy 6'-1", weigh 200 lbs & wear a 40 long coat.

reviews.title: Mostly an Awesome Bag!
reviews.text:
I wish there were some places at the very bottom of the bag so I could lash a cord or whatever attachment through (to attach a tent, sleeping pad, etc) and not have it bounce around. I'm sure the loops are useful for hiking axes or some such thing, but I'd prefer to have a way to not have something flip-flopping around back there. In other words, I'd like a little more reinforced bottom with some possibilities for such a thing, even if it added up to half a pound. ON THE OTHER HAND: I love the hunter green version. Great for camouflage but also has a great attached bright yellow rain fly that you can cover your entire pack with. This will help in emergencies, even if you had to cut it off for a flag, whatever.

I like the top-loading aspect with the very secure extra-gusseted closure that closes underneath the toggle.. Great feature. What I would have liked, also, is something I have in a Kelty pack I bought once: The top portion of that Kelty bag came loose entirely and you could use it as a shoulder bag or fanny/waist pack. It seems like a waste to not have that feature now as it was so useful.

I really like the separate sleeping bag compartment, though there are some gaps where the zipper doesn't go around completely. If you lose some gear, you may have to remove the sleeping bag or whatever and check to see if it didn't sleep down into that compartment. The pros on that feature are that it's easy to get to that without pulling everything out of the pack, or you have a separate compartment for whatever gear you need to get at more easily. Granted you have to take the pack off, but it's a nice option. I'd recommend using a contractor bag or trash bag, etc, to line the bottom of the top section so gear doesn't fall through into the sleeping bag compartment.

The fabric looks to be of good lasting quality and nicely water resistent, though I haven't field tested this yet. Though I'm not used to the idea of having to have to take the aluminum slats out, having a friend help adjust them to the curve of my back, I can see that this can be a great feature. My gripe on that is that, though it'll be a great option, the labels give no directions on how to line those things up to your back when you bend them. Not interested in spending a lot of time experimenting with that once the pack's loaded and I find that it's just not the right fit (yet).

Overall, I give this 5 stars. The price is wonderful and for a person my size (5'6"), it holds the optimal amount of liters/inches of gear and will be versatile enough to play with. It's a great price, and that is very important to me. Not all of us can afford to go it with something from Marmot or whatever, but that doesn't mean we should walk around in pain or with a leaky, ripping, junky bag. A nice medium!

reviews.title: What he needs for Boy Scouts
reviews.text:
We haven't used it yet but was bought for use on Boy Scout trips. My son is 12 yrs old, 63in tall and about 85lbs. the pack fits him well. The sleeping bag compartment holds his compression/mummy style/30 degree sleeping bag, and there's a little room left over. The remaining pack space seems enough for a 2-3 night hike. Some of the plastic snaps do feel a little weak/cheap. Was also looking at the TETON 4000, but I think it would have been a little to big for him.

Update- 9/27/2016
My some used it for a 8 mile hiking trip over the weekend. He is tall and a little skinny. With the waist strap adjusted to the tightest setting we were UNABLE to tighten it enough to transfer the weight off his shoulders and onto his waist. The pack became too heavy and the hike was not a fun experience for him. Also - There are two small mesh pockets on the lower outer side portion of the pack and two larger zippered compartments above the mesh pockets. The boy scouts use the larger pockets to hold water bottles. The pack would be better if the larger pockets were on the bottom and the mesh pockets were in the middle, overall for better weight distribution. And - with items in the pack and in the sleeping bag compartment, the zippers are not easily opened and closed. Overall its a good pack but my son will have to grow into it. I will still keep an eye out for a better pack.

reviews.title: Very small
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:670390d5f4bbbcd1c8a0c687f3650946]] Let me start by saying that I am a person that does a lot of research before buying an item. I don't spend on a whim. It may take me weeks or a month before making that buying commitment. I look for something that has all the features I need.
I was looking to replace my air Jordan sling that I've had for at least 15 years , maybe longer. First choice was another air Jordan but I could not get the same style..so I started hunting.
This brand seemed to fit my needs. Had many of the same feature as my old backpack..and this has a bottle holder which my old one did not have. BONUS :) So I waited till prime day and got this for 10dollars cheaper . Oh ,and the reviews were decent .
Well it arrived today and I couldn't be more disappointed. This is really small for my needs. Measurements on this are embellished. Reviews said this was great for a day trip or hike but honestly if you just wear a Tshirt and shorts you would be ok. I wanted to fit work clothes and my lunch in this bag but that isn't happening.
There are many pockets but let's be honest, you can only fit so much inside this bag. Extra pockets don't give you more space, they provide just separation.
We shall see how the durability of this is. No way it will give me 15 years as I'm pretty rough on my bag as it goes everywhere I go. It's hard letting go of a good pair of shoes or a bag or any fashion you have had for many years cause it's hard to replace something so durable. I'm sure many have had that experience.
Overall this is just to small for my needs as you can see in the picture. The measurements are a stretch. Durability we will see and I will update.
1 WEEK later......no issues with the bag. I have been gentle as far as overstuffing it. The size issue is muy inconvenient. Many issues on that topic but just one......I work outdoors and head to work with a hoodie cause its chilly in the a.m. the hoodie wont fit in the bag when the weather warms and i only have a shirt, lunchbag, phone/charger, misc stuff already in it.. Reading the comments , This makes me curious what kind of day trip people are taking in order to use this bag in that fashion..

If you stuff these really full it’s challenging and awkward (or maybe I’m challenged and awkward) to get the “hood” to the top end and all the straps in place properly. The hood may be an added benefit, but my compression bag for my sleeping bag just has straps up the sides that criss-cross over the top and is very easy.
If you didn’t know, sleeping bags shouldn’t be rolled up, much easier to get in by just taking one corner of sleeping bag and stuffing it in. It will get wrinkled, but hey, it’s camping.
TIP: for gear in regular bags watch you-tube videos on how to tie a compression knot, really handy and inexpensive for some more general items.

reviews.title: Very slender and long
reviews.text:
I did read the measurements - I was cutting it close. I have a surplus sleep system bivy cover and green inner bag I use for cooler weather camp trips. The compression bag that normally comes with it is a bit big for just these two components, so I wanted one a little smaller so I could stuff it into one of the side bags of the motorcycle. I got the small size, the medium was bigger than what I could use. The small was very slender, I had to unroll my sleeping bag, and roll it up from the head down and fold it over in half to fit in this compression sack. I'm glad this bag comes with straps on the sides, I would be afraid the sack might split from the pressure of this sleeping bag. I might update this after the trip is over and I stuff the bag back into this compression sack. Maybe even take a picture with it in the side pannier for size comparison. For size - imagine this; you cannot fit a regular size coffee can over the opening of this sack and have enough fabric to pull over.

Update: After my journey out, and my journey back. For a sleeping bag this size; you have to roll it a few different ways until it works. Fold it in half until its bag width, then roll it as tight as you can. Proof is in the pudding, the bag fits just perfect and still fits into my side bag on the motorcycle. Not really sure what to put into the little pocket on top, maybe an extra pillow cover? Or small flashlight. Hope this picture helps someone decide what they need. I would buy again for other things I need to compress down.

reviews.title: Cute Bag but it smells like…
reviews.text:
I’m in the military and I travel a lot for work. I’m always going from wearing civilian clothes to military uniform. I needed something more versatile while traveling.
This bag is beautiful, I love all the pockets and my computer fits perfectly inside and all it’s accessories while still in regs (took off the tassel attached).
The only thing I did not like was the horrendous smell it came with. I received it the day I was flying out and only had a few hours to try and air it out.
I tried spraying it as well but it still reeks!
I do hope that quality control gets on this and they do something about the smell.
I also hope they take into account that some people are very sensitive to smells.
I mean for $40, this bag shouldn’t be smelling like that.
It’s really bag, like on the spectrum of thrift store and dog/cat pee.

The bag--which includes four padded lumbar back pads for comfort and support--is outfitted with a large main compartment with separate side-entry zippered boot sections, making it easy to slip your boots inside after a long day on the slopes. reviews.title: Incredibly compact and functional
reviews.text:
This pack is small enough to attach to a belt loop or store in a glove compartment. Even after my clumsy job of repacking it into the self-contained and integrated case it measured about 6x6x3 inches and is feather light.

Note that while the product photos (at the time of this review) look as though you are getting a backpack and a smaller case, you are only getting a single item. The smaller case shown is integrated into this pack and functions as the storage when not in use. Since the product description and other reviews have exhaustively covered the physical characteristics I will highlight my experience.

First, there are no real instructions, and when you first figure out how to unpack it you marvel at the full size. Then panic sets in because you cannot believe that you can repack it. Actually, both procedures are fairly intuitive. I had mine unfurled in mere minutes, and when it came time to repack I stretched the entire pack out, folded it over laterally, then continued folding it lengthwise until it fit into the zippered pouch that is part of the pack. Even I was able to accomplish that on the first attempt, and when you've done it a few times it literally takes less than a minute.

Second, it's surprisingly comfortable with the shoulder straps and fit. As others have mentioned it's not for heavy loads (although you can easily manage about twelve pounds without damaging or straining it. The zippers are decent quality and the compartment holds more than I would have guessed. I was about to cram a beach towel, a small bluetooth speaker and a water bottle in the pack with room for other odds and ends.

Overall, this is a great pack for those times when you wish you had one (like at a swap meet or farmer's market), and a grab-and-go for active folks. Even though the design implies occasional use, it can be an everyday pack as long as your loads are relatively light.

reviews.title: A great little portable backpack which is perfect for keeping in the stroller or trunk of your car
reviews.text:
I already owned a little reusable shopping bag that stuffs into its own tiny carrying sack, and I absolutely love it. I keep it in my vehicle, and anytime I happen to be going shopping on the spur of the moment where I don't have my reusable bag, it is already in the car and handy. So when I was offered the opportunity to test this lightweight travel backpack which stuffs into its own small, zippered pouch, I immediately thought about how much handier that would be even than my shopper.

I keep it hanging on my stroller, and because it is a backpack, I can use it for the same purpose as my little reusable shopping bag only when I am out on a walk with my little one, we can stop at the store for a few groceries for that night's dinner, and I don't have to carry the grocery sacks home while trying to manage the stroller. I just pop the groceries into this simple lightweight backpack and go.

The backpack of course is not meant to carry a great deal of weight on long journeys, rather it's meant to be a simple day pack for you to pack a towel, snacks and a bottle of water, or in my case, some meat and vegetables for dinner. I got the black one, and I appreciate that it is stylish and has a streamlined, slim shape that doesn't feel overly bulky.

In order to use the stuff sack, you turn the sack inside out and roll up the backpack into the sack, turning the sack right side out as you do so. This took me some figuring out, since I forgot to pay attention when I initially removed it from the pouch. When you unfold the backpack from the stuff sack, the stuff sack becomes a small zippered pouch within the interior of the backpack which is perfect for keys, lip balm and a credit card.
